hawkish_phrases: raising the ECB's key interest rates by 50 basis points
risks to the inflation outlook continued to be on the upside and had intensified
Inflation had increased further to 8.6% in June
inflationary pressures had broadened and intensified
persistently high inflation posed an increasing risk of longer-term inflation expectations becoming unanchored
dovish_phrases: risks to growth were on the downside
clouding the outlook for the second half of 2022 and beyond
if demand were to weaken over the medium term, it would lower pressures on prices
concerns about disruptions to gas supplies
summary: The Governing Council decided to raise the three key ECB interest rates by 50 basis points (larger than the 25bp previously signaled), with some members dissenting in favor of the smaller 25bp move, citing intensified upside risks to inflation and persistently high inflation at 8.6%, while also approving the new Transmission Protection Instrument to safeguard monetary policy transmission.
